sprint: "Book" Retired From Publish Copy + Read-Screen Corner Radius Fix
task: Two real, user-reported issues from the Publish flow. (1) 'we should not use book any more' -- every user-facing 'book' string in js/publishStudio.js and js/storyDestinations.js is replaced with 'Adventure': the Read screen's CTA (Publish My Book -> Publish My Adventure), the Celebration download button and ready message (Get My Book -> Get My Adventure), the destination-aware Celebration headline, the Almost Ready headline/message, the Make-Another-Story confirm dialog, and the Story Book destination's own display label (Story Book -> Story Adventure). Scoped to the Publish flow only -- internal ids and the separate Theme Designer 'Book Style'/'Book Title' vocabulary are untouched. (2) 'published image has square corners while the one i was building has rounded corners' -- root-caused to a purely cosmetic CSS mismatch: #previewCanvas (the editor) has always used border-radius:var(--card-radius) (16px), while .publish-read-canvas and .publish-celebration-cover had a hardcoded border-radius:6px. Both fixed to match the editor exactly. A new, permanent Creator Governing Rule was added in the same pass: '5. Publish Fidelity -- Publish should honour the runtime view of center pane of Creator,' formalizing that whatever the live editing canvas shows is exactly what Publish must produce, with no separate reconstruction or fallback rendering path ever allowed to diverge from it. Verified via a new Playwright test (book_to_adventure_test.js) confirming the renamed CTA, matching corner radii between the editor and Read canvas, and zero remaining 'book' text anywhere in the Publish Studio modal. Full regression across the existing suite (publish_stages.js's own stale selector updated to match) and both goldenBuild.js suites passes unchanged. No Theme Contract/Engine V2 Scene Model/Builder change; confined to css/style.css, js/publishStudio.js, and js/storyDestinations.js.
